perturbation.original_text: II. PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - Both parties understand and acknowledge that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by stated parties by in vestment of significant time, effort and exposure, and that the confidential information is a valuable, special and unique asset of stated parties which provides each with a significant competitive advantage. Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of each Party.
perturbation.changed_text: II. PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - Both parties understand and acknowledge that the Confidential Information *may* have been developed or obtained by stated parties by investment of significant time, effort and exposure, and that the confidential information *may* be a valuable, special and unique asset of stated parties which *may* provides each with a significant competitive advantage. Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in confidence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of each Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'has been developed' to 'may have been developed' and similarly softening the language about the value and advantage of the information, this now contradicts the mandatory nature of the non-disclosure agreement in the same section. If the information is *potentially* valuable, the *requirement* to protect it becomes questionable, creating in-text contradiction in Section II itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: II. PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - Both parties understand and acknowledge that the Confidential Information *may* have been developed or obtained by stated parties by investment of significant time, effort and exposure, and that the confidential information *may* be a valuable, special and unique asset of stated parties which *may* provides each with a significant competitive advantage. Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of each Party. No copying: Both further agree that they will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of stated parties. No contact: both further agree that they will not directly contact any listed source of Confidential Information without the prior written consent of respective parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts that the confidential information 'has been developed' with significant investment and is a 'valuable, special, and unique asset,' providing a competitive advantage. The changed text weakens these assertions by using 'may have been developed' and 'may be a valuable' asset which 'may' provide competitive advantage. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section II because the 'may' statements conflict with the mandatory requirement to protect the information. If the value and uniqueness of the information are uncertain, it undermines the basis for the strict non-disclosure obligations also found in Section II, making those obligations questionable and difficult to enforce. The contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text, shows the weakened language directly creating the contradiction with the non-disclosure agreement.

perturbation.original_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project.
perturbation.changed_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, and guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project. *However, this non-circumvent clause shall not apply to opportunities independently discovered by either party without the direct assistance or introduction from the other party.*
perturbation.explanation: The original Non-Circumvent clause in Section III is very broad and absolute. By adding the exception that the clause 'shall not apply to opportunities independently discovered', it creates a significant loophole. It creates a contradiction because the original intent was to protect relationships introduced by either party, but now either party could claim an opportunity was 'independently discovered' to circumvent the agreement, contradicting the 'irrevocably agree' and 'guarantee' language at the beginning of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, and guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project. *However, this non-circumvent clause shall not apply to opportunities independently discovered by either party without the direct assistance or introduction from the other party.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the non-circumvent clause, stating it doesn't apply to opportunities independently discovered. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which was to prevent either party from bypassing the other in any introduced relationship. The phrases 'irrevocably agree' and 'guarantee' in the original section (and the contradicted text, which is the same) imply an absolute commitment, which is undermined by the added exception. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as either party could now potentially circumvent the agreement by claiming independent discovery, thus breaking compatibility with the original, absolute non-circumvent commitment.

perturbation.original_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. *However, this restriction does not apply if the Receiving Party can demonstrate that the information was already known to them prior to this agreement.*
perturbation.explanation: Section IV states the agreement is to prevent use of proprietary information. By adding that the restriction doesn't apply if the Receiving Party already knew the information, it creates a large loophole. It directly contradicts the entire purpose of the non-disclosure agreement, as it becomes unenforceable for any information the Receiving Party claims to have already known, regardless of proof. This introduces in-text contradiction because the entire purpose of the agreement is to stop the use of the other party's information, and this completely undermines the original intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. *However, this restriction does not apply if the Receiving Party can demonstrate that the information was already known to them prior to this agreement.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat to the original agreement, stating that the non-disclosure restriction does not apply if the Receiving Party already knew the information. This directly contradicts the purpose of the non-disclosure agreement, which is to protect proprietary information, as it allows the Receiving Party to circumvent the agreement by claiming prior knowledge, making the protection unenforceable and creating an in-text contradiction as the changed text and contradicted text directly conflict with each other.

perturbation.original_text: V. TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that all information shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner.
perturbation.changed_text: V. TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that *only certain information*, specifically *marked and initialed as trade secret before disclosure*, shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting 'only certain information, specifically marked and initialed as trade secret before disclosure', I am contradicting the rest of this section V which states that 'all information shared' is to be held up as proprietary information and a trade secret. Therefore some of the text is implying that only certain information is protected, while the other part of the text, implies that any shared information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that *only certain information*, specifically *marked and initialed as trade secret before disclosure*, shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Initially, section V stated that 'all information shared' is considered proprietary and a trade secret. However, the modification limits this to 'only certain information, specifically marked and initialed as trade secret before disclosure'. This directly contradicts the original blanket statement by creating two conflicting conditions. The contradicted text is located in the same section of the document creating an in-text contradiction.
